name: Al Harewood
abstract: Al Harewood (June 3, 1923 – March 13, 2014) was an American jazz drummer and teacher. He played the drums left-handed.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Manhattan School of Music Contemporary Jazz Ensemble

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Alden
about_quotes.evidence: I know that with the great drummers that I’ve gotten to play with, it’s usually just settled into a great groove, whether it’s Jake or Al Harewood or any of those guys.
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Alden interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Donaldson
about_quotes.evidence: I went on the road with that group, Horace Parlan, George Tucker and Al Harewood
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Donaldson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: Being at Nice one year with Rufus and Al Harewood was kind of the last time that George Wein hired me after that for some reason. I thought “Gee, I finally got a band and it didn’t work out.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: We have a reunion of fellows that came to New York around the same time, 50-some years ago. That would be Curtis Fuller, Cedar Walton, Ron Carter, Al Harewood, and me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: Al Harewood … Al Harewood and I. Helen Merrill. Wonderful singer. She really knows how to … how to play a melody in her voice. Made a nice duo record with her
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Powell
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve been playing with Randy Weston for about the last 15 years... Al Harewood was the drummer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Powell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, July 27, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]
